message.content: Adversaries may exploit the "Escape to Host" technique to break out of a container and gain access to the underlying host, potentially compromising other containerized resources or the host itself. This breach undermines the intended isolation of containerized environments. Common methods include configuring containers to mount the host’s filesystem, using privileged containers to execute commands or load malicious kernel modules, and abusing system calls like unshare and keyctl for privilege escalation. Attackers may also exploit container management sockets (e.g., docker.sock) or vulnerabilities in symbolic links to access the host. Successful exploitation can lead to persistence, lateral movement, or establishing command and control on the host. Notable adversaries using this technique include Doki, Hildegard, Peirates, Siloscape, and TeamTNT. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict container configurations, limiting container privileges, regularly updating and patching container runtimes, and monitoring for abnormal container behaviors.
